arcs.title: The Collector
arcs.summary: An entity that catalogs things-that-remember — living artifacts, creatures with memory, and supernatural witnesses. Killed graduate student Maren Voss eight months ago. Still active in the city. The specimen in the Shockoe Bottom warehouse was hiding from it, and it may have noticed the warehouse activity.
arcs.mc_notes: Players don't know The Collector and the compromised hunter network are potentially separate threats — or are they? The Collector noticed the warehouse. It may have noticed a recent investigator. Petra Holt's principals may have a connection.

arcs.title: The Fae-Adjacent Specimen
arcs.summary: A fae-adjacent creature — old, a things-that-remember entity — slipped into temporal displacement inside an Overwrite pocket in a Shockoe Bottom warehouse. It was sheltered there by Maren Voss's ritual circle because it asked for help; it was hiding from The Collector. Weakness: cannot tolerate unresolved questions. Five-day retrieval deadline active from 2026-04-24.
arcs.mc_notes: Fae/court/monarch lore integration with WoD Supplement v2 still pending — do not proceed with fae contact establishment until lore is confirmed. Specimen carries an unresolved-questions weakness usable against it. 5-day deadline is from 2026-04-24.

arcs.title: Maren Voss's Ritual Circle
arcs.summary: Maren Voss was one of three practitioners who ran a folk-practice ritual in the warehouse. The other two are unnamed and unwarned. They do not know what they started. Maren's dying message: keep them out of it if you can.
arcs.mc_notes: The other two circle members are unknown. Recent investigative searches into Vera Solis, Asha Tremblay, Maren Voss, or Petra Holt may have been noticed — someone is now aware these threads are being pulled.

arcs.title: Petra Holt & Unnamed Principals
arcs.summary: Petra Holt arrived at Gild coffee shop with institutional backing and a six-day retrieval deadline for the specimen. She knows enough to use the word 'containment' correctly. Her unnamed principals are connected to a rival Power faction. If the deadline passes, they become an active problem.
arcs.mc_notes: Petra meeting at Gild was scheduled 9am the day after the most recent session. She agreed without pushing. She did not ask what the name was. Principals unknown — board connection to a rival Power faction.

arcs.title: Consilium Politics & the Engineered Awakening Program
arcs.summary: Dr. Olave holds closed Consilium meetings that exclude some Power faction members — the hierarchy is shifting. Simultaneously, Councilor Marcus Voss runs a secretive program to engineer Awakenings in promising VCU students. Councilor Ingrid Voss is hiding the fact that her own Awakening was engineered decades ago by something she has never fully identified.
arcs.mc_notes: Ingrid Voss's secret is known to at least one outside party who has kept quiet; she owes that party 1 Debt. Nadia Vasek Awakened 8 months ago — timing matches Maren Voss's death. Declan Morrow connection: gave Nadia a Scar on her left palm.

arcs.title: The Compromised Hunter Network
arcs.summary: Someone senior inside a Richmond hunter network accessed an operative's father's operational files and weaponized them to send the operative to kill a protected woman. A parallel inside-investigation is underway. The handler failed to produce the agreed password on the night of the warehouse op — compromised or impostor. A sealed field-research case from the elder Voss may contain the traitor's name.
arcs.mc_notes: Sealed case (Voss's field research) is in the operative's possession. Combination lock. Unopened. Handler couldn't produce password. Unknown text sender knows the operative's name and position. Network knows the operative was on-site at the warehouse the night of the op.

arcs.title: The Tobacco Warehouse Operation
arcs.summary: A tobacco warehouse on the Canal Walk edge of Shockoe Bottom was flagged by the hunter network under suspicious circumstances. Dr. Linh Pham fled it before being intercepted. Whatever was happening inside was not finished when the operative withdrew. It overlaps with the Overwrite activity and the fae specimen site.
arcs.mc_notes: The warehouse flagged by the hunter network is likely the same Shockoe Bottom warehouse a practitioner investigated — confirm in play. Whoever sent the operative there wanted Pham dead. The network address may have been false bait.

arcs.title: The Unknown Text Sender
arcs.summary: An unknown third party texted an operative their own name and warned them they were watching the wrong door on the night of the warehouse op. They have not been responded to. Identity unknown. They know the operative's name and operational position.
arcs.mc_notes: Pending response. Identity fully unknown — could be ally, enemy, or something else. They demonstrated surveillance capability and knowledge of the network.

arcs.title: The Ghost of a Dead Vampire
arcs.summary: A current mark on the monster-poaching market — the ghost of a dead vampire, something that shouldn't exist twice. Whatever it leaves behind is worth money. Not yet engaged. A nameless ghost in the same hub has been mistaken for it once already.
arcs.mc_notes: The hunter is a wanted man in the mortal system. A nameless ghost may be mistaken for this mark — manifested wrong near the hunter once. Market window closes if something else finds it first. The General's coup leverage situation creates a volatile backdrop.

arcs.title: The General's Coup
arcs.summary: A wolf holds damaging information on a vampire faction leader (The General) and is using it to manipulate them with coup bait. The General is volatile. If they call the bluff or move first, the wolf is in serious danger.
arcs.mc_notes: The General's name is TBD. A Debt is owed to The General. Situation is a standoff — the wolf needs to act before the General stops cooperating and starts retaliating.

arcs.title: The Breach in Serrano's Domain
arcs.summary: Something came through a sealed access point in Aldric Serrano's tunnel domain beneath Downtown. He has been defensive and secretive, sealing off entire sections of the canal tunnels. The nature of what breached is unknown.
arcs.mc_notes: Serrano's location is known to Declan Morrow; access costs a Debt. Serrano's domain connects Shockoe Bottom to Downtown via the canal tunnels.

arcs.title: The Overwrite
arcs.summary: A city-wide supernatural phenomenon rewriting physical and historical reality across all of Richmond's hubs. Shockoe Bottom: buildings appear/disappear, street signs shift. Downtown: legal records rewrite themselves. Creighton Court: temporal displacement, different eras coexist. The University: impossible research results, contradictory academic records. Oregon Hill: industrial echoes, phantom work shifts.
arcs.mc_notes: The Revisionists (Mortalis faction in Shockoe Bottom) are cataloguing Overwrite discrepancies rather than correcting them. The same Overwrite signature has been encountered in Shockoe Bottom at least twice. Hollywood Cemetery shows new sections Emmett Daye does not remember, including a grave with a recent date for a living person.

arcs.title: Creighton Court Redevelopment & Temporal Displacement
arcs.summary: RRHA's Renaissance redevelopment is displacing East End families while the Overwrite's temporal displacement anomaly makes different eras of Creighton Court coexist simultaneously. Demolished buildings cast shadows, children play on removed equipment, residents encounter neighbors who died or moved away years ago.
arcs.mc_notes: No PC direct involvement yet. Could connect to the broader Overwrite arc or develop independently.

arcs.title: A Nameless Ghost — Identity & Anchor
arcs.summary: A nameless ghost has no memory of life. An anchor place pulls at him somewhere in Downtown — he hasn't found it. The Overwrite's administrative anomaly in Downtown shifts identities and records, creating a particular danger for a ghost with no name. Vera (NPC medium) can see him clearly and is waiting for something he doesn't know he has.
arcs.mc_notes: Vera owes the ghost 1 Debt. The ghost may be mistaken for a vampire-ghost mark. Finding the anchor/identity may allow him to move on — or may not.
